scenario: Iran FM Araghchi declares Hormuz 'completely open' Apr 17 (Reuters, Al Jazeera); $20B frozen-assets deal under negotiation; Islamabad talks Apr 19; US blockade persists vessel-scoped; Brent $91.87 confirmed (TradingEconomics Apr 17); tail risk 25%; Apr 22 ceasefire expiry is binary catalyst

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat eases on Iran FM Hormuz open declaration and $20B deal signal. Blockade vessel-scoping means non-Iranian fertilizer flows can transit Hormuz. Gulf urea dislocation easing structurally pending formal settlement. Islamabad talks Apr 19 are key catalyst. Source: Barchart ZWK26.

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel demand correlation eases further as Brent retraces to $91.87 on Hormuz open declaration and $20B deal signal. Indonesian ethanol demand trajectory softens. Fertilizer and freight costs remain elevated but directional easing continues with broader risk-premium unwind.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker cost relief as Brent eases to $91.87 reduces freight-driven soy pressure. Indonesian and Vietnamese crush margins improve further. Iran FM Hormuz open declaration and $20B deal framework support continued structural spread compression.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO softens further as Brent crude correlation reverses on Hormuz open declaration. Biodiesel feedstock premium compresses with oil at ~$91.87/bbl. Indonesia's B40 mandate remains intact. Fertilizer disruption for next yield cycle easing on deal signal. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD.

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security pressure continues to ease as freight and energy input costs fall with Brent retrace. Iran FM open declaration supports fertilizer delivery normalization narrative. Q2 planting season fertilizer availability remains structural constraint pending formal Hormuz settlement.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha tracks Brent lower on Hormuz open declaration and $20B deal signal. Blockade vessel-scoping preserves non-Iranian Gulf condensate flow. Qatar force majeure unresolved - full Ras Laffan restart unlikely before late Aug 2026 (The National). Jurong Island and Map Ta Phut feedstock pressure easing.

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene follows naphtha lower on Hormuz open declaration. SABIC and QAPCO export bottlenecks ease as non-Iranian tankers transit Hormuz unimpeded. Agricultural film and irrigation pipe lead times improving. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol eases on Hormuz open declaration. US let Iran oil sanctions waiver expire (Bloomberg Apr 14) tightens enforcement on Iranian methanol exports specifically. Qatar force majeure unresolved. China MTO complexes adjust to lower feedstock trajectory. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea eases on Hormuz open declaration but remains structurally elevated: Qafco force majeure still in effect, Ras Laffan full restart unlikely before late Aug 2026. US waiver expiry tightens Iranian-origin enforcement. Egypt and India continue buying at premium. Supply normalization requires formal Hormuz reopening + P&I restoration. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP eases as Iran FM Hormuz open declaration reduces tail risk of Saudi export dislocation further. Saudi Ma'aden shipping remains disrupted; US Gulf supply continues filling the gap. Wet-season planting pressure in Philippines and Vietnam persists through April.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight costs ease further with Hormuz open declaration as war-risk premiums continue unwinding. Less directly exposed to Hormuz than urea or DAP - freight normalization is the main transmission channel. Palm oil plantations in Malaysia and Indonesia benefit from lower input cost trajectory.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI eases sharply on Iran FM Hormuz open declaration as normalization hopes firm. Non-Iranian tankers transiting Hormuz daily suggests Cape rerouting demand could reverse gradually. Full normalization still depends on P&I restoration post formal reopening + political settlement. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates ease sharply on Hormuz open declaration and $20B deal signal. US blockade scope confirmed narrow; commercial Hormuz flows partially preserved. Full P&I restoration requires formal reopening + political settlement - Apr 22 ceasefire expiry is the key catalyst. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: Container rates ease sharply on Hormuz open declaration. Asia-Europe rates remain above pre-crisis levels as Suez normalization requires P&I restoration and formal Hormuz reopening. Southeast Asian manufacturers see improved lead-time expectations on Islamabad talks Apr 19. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (est.).
